title: School and Learning Game Zone
source: Generated from approved unit runtime and linked master dictionary

games.title: Meaning Match
games.description: Match each unit word to its approved child-friendly meaning.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: A person who helps us learn new things.
rounds.answer: teacher
rounds.explanation: teacher: A person who helps us learn new things.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: The rules for how we put words together to speak and write.
rounds.answer: grammar
rounds.explanation: grammar: The rules for how we put words together to speak and write.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: A game or test to see who is best.
rounds.answer: contest
rounds.explanation: contest: A game or test to see who is best.

games.title: Spelling Builder
games.description: Choose letter tiles to build important unit words.

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: A person who writes books or stories.
rounds.answer: author

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: A time when we learn something new in school.
rounds.answer: lesson

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: A short paper or talk that tells about something.
rounds.answer: report

games.title: Sentence Puzzle
games.description: Put unit words into the correct sentence order.

rounds.prompt: Build the complete model sentence.
rounds.answer: My teacher helps me with my mathematics homework.

rounds.prompt: Build the complete model sentence.
rounds.answer: I go to the library to read and borrow books.

rounds.prompt: Build the complete model sentence.
rounds.answer: We learn grammar so we can write correctly.

games.title: Language Choice
games.description: Choose the form that makes the unit language accurate.

rounds.prompt: Which word joins these two ideas: “I like the library ______ it is quiet.”?
rounds.answer: because
rounds.explanation: Because introduces a reason; but shows contrast, so that shows purpose, and even though shows surprise.

rounds.prompt: In ‘The Grammar Champions’, where did Amal’s group go to study and write their report?
rounds.answer: To the school library
rounds.explanation: The story says the group met in the library the next day to study together and write their report.

rounds.prompt: Which word means small pieces of information about something?
rounds.answer: details
rounds.explanation: Supply names objects, topic names the idea you write about, and education names learning itself.

games.title: Reading Detective
games.description: Use unit evidence to solve each reading clue.

rounds.prompt: Which subject does Adam like best, and what reason does he give?
choices: Adam likes grammar best because it helps him write well.
The singer learns in the library, which the song calls quiet and bright.
Maya brought her book and her eraser.
rounds.answer: Adam likes grammar best because it helps him write well.
rounds.explanation: Review the unit text evidence: Adam likes grammar best because it helps him write well.

rounds.prompt: What is the name of Amal’s group, and which topic did they choose?
choices: The singer learns in the library, which the song calls quiet and bright.
The word studied tells us that it happened in the past.
Her group is called The Grammar Champions, and they chose prepositions.
rounds.answer: Her group is called The Grammar Champions, and they chose prepositions.
rounds.explanation: Review the unit text evidence: Her group is called The Grammar Champions, and they chose prepositions.

rounds.prompt: Which two things did Maya bring to school that morning?
choices: It shows that he values school and enjoys learning enough to want to teach others.
Maya brought her book and her eraser.
Her group is called The Grammar Champions, and they chose prepositions.
rounds.answer: Maya brought her book and her eraser.
rounds.explanation: Review the unit text evidence: Maya brought her book and her eraser.

games.title: Speaking Quest
games.description: Listen, record, review and submit useful unit language.

rounds.prompt: Speaking 1 — Talk About Your School Day
Speaking 3 — Guess My Subject
rounds.target: In this subject, we work with numbers.

rounds.prompt: Speaking 5 — Explain and Respond
rounds.target: I study in the library because it is quiet.

games.title: Word Order Race
games.description: Arrange unit language in a clear, accurate order.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: I study my spelling words every night.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: We had a spelling contest at school.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: I added more details to my report.

games.title: Definition Dash
games.description: Choose the approved meaning before moving to the next clue.

rounds.prompt: What does 'lesson' mean in this unit?
choices: A time when we learn something new in school.
A person who helps us learn new things.
A person who writes books or stories.
rounds.answer: A time when we learn something new in school.
rounds.explanation: lesson: A time when we learn something new in school.

rounds.prompt: What does 'topic' mean in this unit?
choices: A person who helps us learn new things.
A person who writes books or stories.
The subject or idea we talk or write about.
rounds.answer: The subject or idea we talk or write about.
rounds.explanation: topic: The subject or idea we talk or write about.

rounds.prompt: What does 'author' mean in this unit?
choices: A place where you can find and borrow books.
A person who writes books or stories.
A person who helps us learn new things.
rounds.answer: A person who writes books or stories.
rounds.explanation: author: A person who writes books or stories.

games.title: Word Type Power
games.description: Identify how each vocabulary word works in English.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'prepare'?
rounds.answer: Verb
rounds.explanation: prepare is used as a verb in this dictionary sense.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'supply'?
rounds.answer: Noun
rounds.explanation: supply is used as a noun in this dictionary sense.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'mathematics'?
rounds.answer: Noun
rounds.explanation: mathematics is used as a noun in this dictionary sense.

games.title: Memory Pairs
games.description: Reveal tiles and connect each word with its meaning.

rounds.prompt: Match vocabulary set 1.
pairs: A place where you can find and borrow books.
To finish school or a class.
The rules for how we put words together to speak and write.

rounds.prompt: Match vocabulary set 2.
pairs: Learning about things at school or at home.
A game or test to see who is best.

rounds.prompt: Match vocabulary set 3.
pairs: The subject or idea we talk or write about.
Things we use at school or home, like pencils and paper.
A person who helps us learn new things.

games.title: Question Quest
games.description: Read each unit question carefully and choose the best answer.

rounds.prompt: Which sentence uses the simple past tense correctly?
choices: She prepare her report last night.
He goed to the library on Monday morning.
We studied our spelling words twice.
They eats rice for dinner yesterday.
rounds.answer: We studied our spelling words twice.
rounds.explanation: Study is a regular verb, so it adds –ed; the others need prepared, went and ate.

rounds.prompt: Which question correctly asks about something that happened in the past?
choices: What did you learned in class?
Did he went to the library yesterday?
When you did start the lesson?
rounds.answer: Where did the contest happen?
rounds.explanation: After did we keep the base verb, and did must come before the subject, not after it.

rounds.prompt: Which sentence shows the possessive ’s used correctly?
rounds.answer: The teacher’s desk is tidy.
rounds.explanation: One owner takes an apostrophe and then s, attached to the owner rather than to the thing owned.

games.title: Unit Mission
games.description: Finish with a mixed challenge across the whole unit.

rounds.prompt: Which word means a place where you can find and borrow books?
rounds.answer: library
rounds.explanation: A library is a place, while an eraser is a tool, a contest is an event, and mathematics is a subject.

rounds.prompt: What does the word ‘author’ mean?
choices: A tool used to clean pencil marks from paper.
A short paper or talk that tells about something.
Things we use at school or home, like pencils and paper.
A person who writes books or stories.
rounds.answer: A person who writes books or stories.
rounds.explanation: The other three options are the meanings of eraser, report and supply.

rounds.prompt: In ‘A Day at School’, why does Adam say grammar is his favourite subject?
choices: Because it helps him write well.
Because it happens after lunch every single day.
rounds.answer: Because it helps him write well.
rounds.explanation: Adam gives that exact reason right after the word because in the passage.
